name: Time-restricted feeding (16:8)
description: Compress eating to an 8-hour window (e.g. 12:00-20:00). Water, salt water, black coffee, plain tea allowed outside window.
rationale: Extends the period of low insulin each day, deepening ketosis and improving metabolic flexibility. Easy on keto because hunger drops.

name: Extended fast (24-72h)
description: Periodic longer fast - one 24h every 2-4 weeks, optional 48-72h once per quarter if well adapted.
rationale: Clears glycogen completely, amplifies autophagy, breaks weight-loss plateaus. Not essential but a high-leverage tool once fat-adapted.

name: Post-meal walk
description: 10-20 min slow walk after the largest meal of the day.
rationale: Muscle glucose uptake during light activity blunts post-meal glucose and insulin, preserving ketosis. Works even on non-keto days.

name: Resistance training
description: 2-4 strength sessions per week. Compound lifts with adequate rest. Plug into the parakeet program.
rationale: Preserves (and can grow) lean mass during caloric deficit; improves insulin sensitivity; essential counterweight to low-carb muscle protein balance.

name: Low-impact cardio (walking / cycling / swimming)
description: 60+ minutes low-intensity zone-2 cardio daily, accumulated across the day.
rationale: Higher fat oxidation rate than high-intensity work; does not spike cortisol; safe on affected lipedema limbs (no running/jumping).

name: Skip high-intensity work weeks 1-4
description: HIIT, heavy plyometrics, long hard intervals off the table during adaptation phase.
rationale: Glycogen-dependent; poorly tolerated until fat adaptation is complete. Impact also aggravates lipedema tissue.

name: Sleep hygiene
description: 7-9 hours, consistent schedule, cool dark room, no screens 1h pre-bed. Magnesium glycinate can help.
rationale: Sleep debt spikes insulin resistance and cortisol - both torpedo keto. Glymphatic/lymphatic clearance also peaks in deep sleep.

name: Morning sunlight exposure
description: 5-15 min outside within first hour of waking, ideally without sunglasses.
rationale: Anchors circadian rhythm, improves night-time melatonin release, supports sleep quality that keto depends on.

name: Cold exposure (optional)
description: Cold shower or cold dip 2-5 min, 2-3x/week. Not mandatory.
rationale: Activates brown adipose; modest cofactor for fat oxidation. Bigger anti-inflammatory lever than fat-loss lever.

name: Stress management practice
description: Meditation, breathwork, yoga, journaling - 10-20 min daily consistency over intensity.
rationale: Chronic cortisol elevation raises glucose via liver output and worsens insulin resistance - directly antagonises keto. Lipedema inflammation amplifies with stress too.

name: Diaphragmatic / vagal breathing
rationale: Parasympathetic activation; also massages the thoracic duct (main lymphatic return), overlapping RAD lymphatic benefit.

name: Track blood ketones (first 8 weeks)
description: AM fasted BHB via finger-prick meter during fat-adaptation phase; taper off once stable in 0.5-3.0 range.
rationale: The only reliable ketosis signal. Urine strips stop working after 2-3 weeks; breath acetone meters are noisy. Finger-prick strips are the gold standard.
